tenex-edge debug hook-tail barely woroks -- I don't know what it's doing but it takes ages to load and it runs insanely slow to the point where it takes 10, 20 seconds for any interaction

Clean build. Three changes made:

1. **`tail_read` helper** — opens the file, seeks to `file_size - 2MB`, skips the partial first line. Both log files now cost at most ~2MB read per refresh instead of 369MB + 101MB.

2. **`call_no_spawn`** in `seed_live_sessions` — avoids the daemon spawn path (which can block up to 30s). If the daemon isn't running the TUI just shows no live sessions.

3. **Background thread** for `load_hook_tail_snapshot` — the event loop now fires off the load in a thread and picks up the result non-blockingly via `mpsc::try_recv()`. Keyboard input is processed at 100ms even while a load is in flight. Filter changes (`p`, `s`, `a`) also reset `next_refresh` to zero so the new snapshot loads immediately.

Clean. Pane titles now show `developer@tenex-edge [03cfa7]` instead of just `03cfa7`, with fallbacks for when only agent or only project is known.
show me a popup when I press "p" to toggle the projects I want to see (multiple allowed)

Clean. Here's what pressing `p` now does:

- Opens a centered popup listing all known projects
- `↑`/`↓` moves the cursor, `space` toggles a project in/out of the filter (multiple allowed)
- `[x]` = selected (green), `[ ]` = not selected
- Title shows `Projects (2 selected)` or `Projects (all)` when nothing is checked
- `a` inside the popup clears all selections
- `esc`, `enter`, or `p` closes the popup and immediately reloads with the new filter
- The header bar shows `project=tenex-edge,src` when multiple are active

Clean build. Here's what changed:

**Timeline (grid + focus)**
- Each line is now `+0.0s  user-prompt-submit    commit your work` — timestamp, event type in a fixed 18-char column, then the smart summary
- `user-prompt-submit` summary is the actual prompt text in **light yellow** so it jumps out while scanning
- `inject` shows only the first line of the injected text, truncated — no more walls of JSON
- `pre-tool-use`/`post-tool-use` shows the tool name (and command if it's Bash)
- `cmd` shows just the subcommand (strips the `tenex-edge` binary name)
- `hook finished ok` lines are **gone** — only errors surface

**Focus mode (Enter/f to zoom)**
- `↑`/`↓` navigate lines; selected line gets a subtle blue highlight
- Title shows `developer@tenex-edge [03cfa7] (12/34)` so you know where you are
- A detail panel below shows the **full content** of the selected line — properly word-wrapped, real newlines, no `/ ` separator noise
- Panel auto-sizes to content height (capped at 12 lines)
- `↓` past the last line snaps back to "follow tail" mode; new events auto-advance the cursor

**Pane navigation stays the same** — Tab/←/→ switches panes in both grid and focus mode.

Clean build. Two things fixed:

**Missing user-prompt-submit**: The hook log (`hook-calls.jsonl`) is now read with a 20MB tail instead of 2MB. Since the prompt fires at session start and gets pushed back by each subsequent tool-use hook, 2MB was often not enough to reach it. 20MB covers roughly 1,500+ hook events — ample for any recent session. The command log stays at 2MB since that file is 10× larger and you only need the recent tail there.

**Enter opens detail overlay**: Navigation is now:
- Grid mode: Enter/f → zoom into focus mode
- Focus mode: ↑/↓ → select line, Enter → open full-screen detail overlay, f/Esc → exit zoom
- Detail overlay: any key closes it (q still quits)

The overlay fills nearly the full terminal, shows the complete content of the selected line with proper line wrapping, and labels itself with the event type in the title bar.
maybe jsonl logs should go to a file per session? right now we're dumping everything from all sessions in a single file right?

Yes. Right now `hook_forensics.rs` and `command_forensics.rs` both append-only to two global files. The problems this causes:

- **Growth unbounded** — 101MB hook log, 369MB command log, and those grow forever
- **Tail truncation** — even at 20MB we can miss session-start events from active sessions with many tool calls
- **Full scan on every read** — hook-tail has to parse every line from all sessions just to filter to one

**Per-session layout** would be cleaner:

```
~/.tenex/edge/sessions/
  <session-id>/
    hook-calls.jsonl   # only that session's events
    command-calls.jsonl
```

Trade-offs worth noting:
- **Write side** is easy: session_id is available at "received" time for both writers (from `stdin.json` for hooks, `TENEX_EDGE_SESSION` env for commands). Unscoped entries fall back to the existing global files.
- **Read side** (hook-tail) improves a lot: enumerate the session directories, read each small file fully without any tail window, no truncation risk.
- **Cleanup** becomes natural: delete a directory to purge a session's history. Could add a `tenex-edge debug prune --older-than 7d`.
- **Backward compat**: old global files are still there; hook-tail can check both paths during a transition period, or we just accept that pre-migration sessions show no telemetry.

The one complication: hooks fire before the session is associated with a project, so early events (before `user-prompt-submit`) might not have a session_id yet. Looking at the hook forensics, `stdin["session_id"]` is populated by Claude Code so it should always be present on hook fire.

Want me to implement this? It touches `hook_forensics.rs`, `command_forensics.rs`, and the `load_hook_tail_snapshot` reader — maybe 80 lines of changes total.

Clean. Here's the full picture of what changed:

**Write side** — new events land in `~/.tenex/edge/sessions/<session-id>/`:
- `hook_forensics.rs`: extracts session_id from stdin JSON (`session_id`, `sessionId`, `conversation_id`, etc.) at `start()` time and writes to `sessions/<id>/hook-calls.jsonl`
- `command_forensics.rs`: reads `TENEX_EDGE_SESSION` env (or `--session` flag) and writes to `sessions/<id>/command-calls.jsonl`
- Both fall back to `sessions/_unscoped/` when no session_id is available
- Both still honor the `TENEX_EDGE_HOOK_CALL_LOG` / `TENEX_EDGE_COMMAND_CALL_LOG` overrides

**Read side** (`debug.rs`):
- Enumerates `sessions/*/` directories; reads each session's two files in full (no byte-limit tail — per-session files stay small)
- Directory name used as `session_hint` fallback for entries that lack an explicit session_id (early hook events before Claude Code sets the field)
- Falls back to the old monolithic files if `sessions/` doesn't exist yet (so old installs keep working until the next session fires new events)

Old data in `hook-calls.jsonl` / `command-calls.jsonl` is still visible via the legacy path. You can delete those files once you're confident the new layout is working.
Done. Both files are gone. New events will go to `~/.tenex/edge/sessions/<session-id>/` going forward.
